HOOK · Changes of State (Six Changes)
状态变化（六种）
HOOK
•
Hold an ice cube on a warm hand and it melts.
把冰块放在温暖的手上,它会化掉。
•
Leave a wet floor and the water seems to vanish into the air.
湿地板放着不管,水好像消失到空气里。
•
Breathe on a cold window and tiny drops appear.
对着冷玻璃哈气,会出现小水珠。
•
In every case a substance is changing state — solid, liquid, gas — without changing what it is made of.
每一次都是一种物质在改变状态——固、液、气——但它是什么东西没有变。
•
This lesson asks: what is happening to the particles each time a substance changes state?
这一课问:每次物质改变状态时,粒子身上发生了什么?
LEARN · Changes of State (Six Changes)
状态变化（六种）
LEARN
Naming the six changes
•
Six changes of state, each with a name:
六种状态变化,每种都有名字:
•
melting: solid -> liquid
熔化 (melting):固 -> 液
•
freezing: liquid -> solid
凝固 (freezing):液 -> 固
•
evaporation / boiling: liquid -> gas
蒸发 / 沸腾 (evaporation / boiling):液 -> 气
•
condensation: gas -> liquid
凝结 (condensation):气 -> 液
•
sublimation: solid -> gas (directly)
升华 (sublimation):固 -> 气(直接)
•
deposition: gas -> solid (directly)
凝华 (deposition):气 -> 固(直接)
Particle-level cause
•
Changing state does not change the particles themselves; it changes their energy, motion and arrangement.
改变状态不会改变粒子本身;改变的是粒子的能量、运动和排列。
•
Heating (gaining energy): particles move faster, the forces between them are overcome, particles move further apart -> melting, evaporation, boiling, sublimation.
加热(得到能量):粒子动得更快,克服彼此之间的力,粒子分得更开
•
Cooling (losing energy): particles move more slowly, the forces between them pull them closer, particles move closer together -> condensation, freezing, deposition.
> 熔化、蒸发、沸腾、升华。
•
冷却(失去能量):粒子动得更慢,彼此之间的力把它们拉得更近,粒子靠得更近 -> 凝结、凝固、凝华。
🔴 RED CARD · 误区红牌
✘
When a substance melts or boils, its particles get bigger or change into something new.
物质熔化或沸腾时,它的粒子会变大或变成新的东西。
✔
The particles stay exactly the same size and the same substance. Only their energy, spacing and movement change.
粒子大小完全不变,还是同一种物质。变的只是它们的能量、间距 和运动。
Safety + boundary
•
Hot ice and boiling water experiments must be supervised by a teacher.
安全:热冰和沸水实验必须有老师看着。
•
Do not carry them out alone.
不要自己一个人做。
•
NOTE (for later study): the exact amount of energy needed to melt or boil a substance can be calculated with a formula.
提醒(以后才学):熔化或沸腾一种物质需要的确切能量,可以用一个公式算出来。
•
That calculation is studied in a later year, not here.
那个计算以后年级才学,这里不学。
TRY · Changes of State (Six Changes)
状态变化（六种）
TRY
Q01Complete / Draw5 marks
This question is about states of matter. (a) Complete the sentence using the correct states of matter. When a substance condenses, it changes from a .................. to a .................. . [1] (b) In the box below, draw how the particles would appear in a liquid. Draw at least six more particles. The first particle has been drawn for you. [1] (c) The table shows the melting points and boiling points of three common substances. Choose a substance from the table which is: a gas at 10 deg C .................. a solid at 100 deg C .................. a liquid over the smallest range of temperatures .................. [3]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a) gas to liquid (accept: vapour to liquid); (b) at least 6 more particles; close together / touching; random; arrangement; (c) nitrogen; iron; nitrogen (accept: N2; Fe).
(a) gas to liquid (accept: vapour to liquid); (b) at least 6 more particles; close together / touching; random; arrangement; (c) nitrogen; iron; nitrogen (accept: N2; Fe).
Accept · 可收：
  • (a) gas to liquid (accept: vapour to liquid)
  • (b) at least 6 more particles
  • close together / touching
  • random
  • arrangement
  • (c) nitrogen
  • iron
  • nitrogen (accept: N2
Q02Draw lines / Use / Draw an arrow4 marks
(a) Farid has three different fuels. Draw lines between each fuel and the correct state of matter. [1] (b)(i) Solids, liquids and gases can change from one state of matter to another. Use the words in the list to write the names of the processes shown in the diagram. condensation evaporation freezing sublimation [2] (b)(ii) Draw an arrow (->) on the diagram to show what happens during the process of melting. [1]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a) official matching: bags / pipe / cylinders; pipe -> gas; (b)(i) freezing; evaporation; (b)(ii) arrow drawn solid -> liquid (melting).
(a) official matching: bags / pipe / cylinders; pipe -> gas; (b)(i) freezing; evaporation; (b)(ii) arrow drawn solid -> liquid (melting).
Accept · 可收：
  • (a) official matching: bags / pipe / cylinders
  • pipe -> gas
  • (b)(i) freezing
  • evaporation
  • (b)(ii) arrow drawn solid -> liquid (melting)
  • (a) official matching: bags / pipe / cylinders; pipe -> gas; (b)(i) freezing; evaporation; (b)(ii) arrow drawn solid -> liquid (melting)
  • evaporate the water
  • heat it to remove water
Q03Choose / Complete3 marks
A boy sets up the experiment shown below to investigate the properties of ethanol, a colourless liquid. He heats the water gently and stirs with the thermometer. When the temperature reaches 78 deg C, he notices that the ethanol bubbles vigorously and disappears. Choose words from the list to complete the explanation below. boils colour dissolves energy melts molecules state temperature Ethanol gains .................. from the hot water. When the .................. reaches 78 deg C, the ethanol .................. and changes from a liquid to a gas. [3]
Expected answer · 标准答案
energy.
energy.
Accept · 可收：
  • energy
  • temperature
  • boils
  • energy.
  • 温度。
  • boils.
Q04Choose / Describe4 marks
The diagram shows how the three states of matter can be changed into each other. Choose one word from the list to describe each change. condense dissolve evaporate freeze melt (a) P .................. [1] (b) Q .................. [1] (c) R .................. [1] (d) S .................. [1]
Expected answer · 标准答案
P = condense.
P = condense.
Accept · 可收：
  • P = condense
  • Q = evaporate
  • R = freeze
  • S = melt
  • (accept: condensation / evaporation / freezing / melting)
  • P = condense.
  • Q = evaporate.
TEST · Changes of State (Six Changes)
状态变化（六种）
EXAM MODE · NO HINTS · 考试模式
Q05Tick / Draw / Describe5 marks
Copper is a metal. The diagram shows the arrangement of particles in solid copper at 25 deg C. Copper melts at 1084 deg C and boils at 2562 deg C. (a) What is the state of matter of copper at 1000 deg C? Tick the correct answer. [1] (b) What is the state of matter of copper at 2000 deg C? Tick the correct answer. [1] (c) Draw the arrangement of particles of copper in copper gas. [1] (d) Describe the changes in the motion and the arrangement of particles in solid copper when it melts. change in motion .................. change in arrangement .................. [2]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a) solid; (b) liquid; (c) gas particles far apart and random; (d) motion: move faster / move around, not only vibrate.
(a) 固体; (b) 液体; (c) 气体 粒子 相距很远 and 随机; (d) motion: 移动更快 / move around, not only 振动。
Accept · 可收：
  • (a) solid
  • (b) liquid
  • (c) gas particles far apart and random
  • (d) motion: move faster / move around, not only vibrate
  • (a) solid; (b) liquid; (c) gas particles far apart and random; (d) motion: move faster / move around, not only vibrate
  • (a) 固体。
  • (b) 液体。
Q06Tick4 marks
Baking powder is used to help cakes rise. The chemical name for baking powder is sodium hydrogencarbonate. When heated, sodium hydrogencarbonate forms sodium carbonate, water and carbon dioxide. (b) The gas that forms helps the cake to rise. Which of these sentences are true? Put ticks in the boxes next to the correct sentences. [2] (c) Sodium hydrogencarbonate is a solid. Which of these sentences are true? Put ticks in the boxes next to the correct sentences. [2]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(b) tick: gas particles fill all the space; gas particles move in; random directions; (c) tick: solid particles packed close; solid particles vibrate.
(b) tick: gas particles fill all the space; gas particles move in; random directions; (c) tick: solid particles packed close; solid particles vibrate.
Accept · 可收：
  • (b) tick: gas particles fill all the space
  • gas particles move in
  • random directions
  • (c) tick: solid particles packed close
  • solid particles vibrate
  • (b) tick: gas particles fill all the space; gas particles move in; random directions; (c) tick: solid particles packed close; solid particles vibrate
  • (b) tick: gas particles fill all the space.
Q07Name1 mark
(c) Solids can change state to become gases. Look at the diagram to show the changes in state. solid --- process A ---> liquid --- process B ---> gas Name processes A and B. A .................. B .................. [1] [RESERVED IMAGE SLOT — model-answer sketch, no source diagram supplied; to be drawn at build]
Expected answer · 标准答案
A = melting.
A = 熔化。
Accept · 可收：
  • A = melting
  • B = evaporating / boiling
  • A = 熔化。
  • B = evaporating / 沸腾。
Q08Choose / Explain5 marks
This question is about states of matter. (a) Complete these sentences about states of matter. Choose words from the list. be squashed break condense expand melt separate The particles in solids are close together which means solids cannot .................. . When a liquid evaporates the particles .................. . When a solid changes into a liquid it is said to .................. . [3] (b) The particles of a gas fill any container in which they are placed. Explain why. [2]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a) be squashed; separate; melt; (b) gas particles fill the container because they are moving /; spreading out / far apart / move anywhere.
(a) be squashed; separate; melt; (b) gas particles fill the container because they are moving /; spreading out / far apart / move anywhere.
Accept · 可收：
  • (a) be squashed
  • separate
  • melt
  • (b) gas particles fill the container because they are moving /
  • spreading out / far apart / move anywhere
  • (a) be squashed; separate; melt; (b) gas particles fill the container because they are moving /; spreading out / far apart / move anywhere
  • (a) be squashed.
Q09Tick / Describe4 marks
(a) Tick the boxes next to the two correct sentences about gases. [2] (b) A gas changes into a liquid during condensation. Describe what happens to the particles in a substance during condensation. [2]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a) tick: gases can flow to fill a container; gas particles are; arranged randomly; (b) any two: lose energy / move slower; forces increase; particles; get closer / distance smaller.
(a) tick: gases can flow to fill a container; gas particles are; arranged randomly; (b) any two: lose energy / move slower; forces increase; particles; get closer / distance smaller.
Accept · 可收：
  • (a) tick: gases can flow to fill a container
  • gas particles are
  • arranged randomly
  • (b) any two: lose energy / move slower
  • forces increase
  • particles
  • get closer / distance smaller
  • (a) tick: gases can flow to fill a container; gas particles are; arranged randomly; (b) any two: lose energy / move slower; forces increase; particles; get closer / distance smaller
Q10Complete4 marks
Chen draws a diagram to show water changing from a liquid to water vapour. Complete the sentences. All the water molecules in the liquid are moving. Some of the molecules have more .................. . These molecules move .................. enough to escape the surface of the liquid. This is called .................. . This makes the liquid become .................. . [4]
Expected answer · 标准答案
energy.
energy.
Accept · 可收：
  • energy
  • fast / quick / rapid
  • evaporation
  • cooler
  • energy.
  • fast / quick / rapid.
  • 蒸发。
WRAP · Changes of State (Six Changes)
状态变化（六种）
WRAP
📝 SUMMARY · 总结
•
Six changes of state, one rule:
六种状态变化,一条规律:
•
Heating gives particles energy -> they move apart
加热给粒子能量 -> 粒子分开(熔化、蒸发、沸腾、升华)。
•
(melting, evaporation, boiling, sublimation).
冷却拿走能量 -> 粒子靠拢(凝结、凝固、凝华)。
•
Cooling takes energy away -> particles come together
粒子本身从不改变;变的只是能量、间距和运动。
•
(condensation, freezing, deposition).
下一课:当我们稳定地加热或冷却一种物质,把温度随时间画出来时,状态变化会在图上表现为一段段平的线。
•
The particles themselves never change; only their energy, spacing and movement do.
那就是加热 / 冷却曲线。
•
Next: when we heat or cool a substance steadily and plot temperature over time, the changes of state appear as flat sections on a graph.
•
That is the heating / cooling curve.
🧠 MEMORY HOOK · 口诀金句
Heat up: melt/boil; Cool down: condense/freeze.
记忆法：Heat up: melt/boil; Cool down: condense/freeze。
🔁 COVER & RECALL · 盖住回想
Chen draws a diagram to show water changing from a liquid to water vapour. Complete the sentences. All the water molecules in the liquid are moving. Some of the
energy.
energy.

嗨!准备好了吗?
